agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A new 'auto-split' feature is proposed that would automatically divide shipments exceeding 500 kg into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different carrier, bypassing supervisor involvement. This directly conflicts with two established production invariants in the existing domain model.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for physically transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept — a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment. Does not exist in the current domain model."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The binding of exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once set, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the sole authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic process to break a Shipment into multiple sub-shipments when weight exceeds 500 kg, assigning different carriers without Supervisor approval."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of sending a Shipment for delivery; the system guarantees a Carrier is assigned at this point."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data integrity constraint. A Shipment with zero or more than one Carrier is in a corrupt state — accountability, tracking, liability, and billing all depend on a single Carrier per Shipment. If violated, the system cannot answer 'who is responsible for this shipment.'","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in any non-terminal state, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization and state integrity constraint. Carrier reassignment has liability, contractual, and chain-of-custody implications. Allowing unsupervised reassignment breaks the audit trail and removes human accountability for a high-impact decision.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment event must have an associated Supervisor identity in the audit log. Reassignment attempts without Supervisor authorization must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier assigned.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the dispatch precondition. A Shipment cannot physically leave without a single accountable Carrier. This is a physical and legal reality — someone must sign for custody.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation must fail if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A 501 kg shipment is auto-split into two sub-shipments, each assigned a different carrier. The original Shipment now has zero carriers or two carriers, violating BR-1.","expected_behavior":"Cannot be determined — this is the core contradiction."},{"scenario":"Auto-split reassigns carriers without Supervisor involvement, violating BR-2.","expected_behavior":"Cannot be determined — this is the core contradiction."},{"scenario":"A shipment is 500 kg exactly — does it split or not? Boundary ambiguity.","expected_behavior":"Unspecified — the feature request says 'exceeds 500 kg' so 500 kg exactly should not split, but this needs explicit confirmation."},{"scenario":"A shipment is 1500 kg and must be split into 3+ sub-shipments. What is the splitting strategy? Equal weight? Bin-packing by carrier capacity?","expected_behavior":"Unspecified in the feature request."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment's carrier becomes unavailable after the split. Who has authority to reassign — does the Supervisor rule apply to sub-shipments?","expected_behavior":"Unspecified — depends on resolving the contradiction first."},{"scenario":"The parent shipment has already been assigned a carrier and is locked. Auto-split would effectively unassign that carrier from the original shipment.","expected_behavior":"This is a reassignment without Supervisor approval — violates BR-2."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"If the contradiction is not resolved and the feature ships with broken invariants, liability gaps emerge: when a sub-shipment is lost or damaged, there is no clear single accountable party on the original shipment. Contractual disputes between multiple carriers become likely.","operational":"Introducing sub-shipments without updating the domain model creates a parallel tracking structure. Dispatch, billing, proof-of-delivery, and customer notifications all assume one Carrier per Shipment. Every downstream system must be audited.","user":"Customers currently track a single shipment with a single carrier. Splitting introduces multiple tracking threads, potential confusion about delivery times, and split-delivery coordination problems.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping two contradictory invariants into production risks data corruption, audit trail gaps, and legal liability exposure. The Supervisor bypass removes a human safeguard that exists for a reason.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle a new 'sub-shipment' concept. Dispatch workflow changes. Current tooling assumes 1:1 Shipment:Carrier."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their authority over carrier reassignment is being bypassed for a class of shipments. This undermines the control structure they are accountable for."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"A carrier assigned to a shipment may find that shipment auto-split and partially reassigned to a competitor without any human review. Contractual implications."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single shipment becomes multiple deliveries. Customer-facing tracking, ETAs, and delivery coordination all change."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Billing assumes one carrier per shipment. Split shipments need a new billing model — who pays what, how are carrier fees split, how is the customer invoiced."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Chain-of-custody, liability, and insurance all assume a single carrier per shipment. Sub-shipments create ambiguity in liability allocation."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction between the proposed feature and two established production invariants (one-carrier-per-shipment and supervisor-only reassignment). Until the product team resolves whether to (a) redefine the invariants, (b) model sub-shipments as first-class entities with their own carrier assignments, or (c) require supervisor approval for auto-split, no acceptance criteria can be confidently defined.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split and the existing invariants is unambiguous","The existing domain model invariants are well-defined and in production"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends sub-shipments to be first-class Shipment entities or a new aggregate","Whether the 500 kg threshold is a hard business constraint or a configurable policy","Whether the Supervisor bypass is intentional or an oversight in the feature request","The splitting strategy (equal weight, carrier capacity, geographic optimization)"],"assumptions":["The existing invariants as stated are accurately representing the production system","The feature request represents a first draft and has not been reviewed against the existing domain model"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly violates BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment). Splitting a shipment into sub-shipments each handled by a different carrier means either: (a) the parent shipment no longer has exactly one carrier, or (b) sub-shipments are new entities that need their own domain definition. The feature request does not introduce the concept of a sub-shipment as a first-class domain entity, so it implicitly breaks the one-carrier invariant.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Carriers","Customers","Finance","Legal"],"options":["A: Introduce 'Sub-shipment' as a new aggregate root, each with its own single Carrier assignment. The parent Shipment becomes a 'Shipment Group' or 'Composite Shipment' — a coordination entity, not a dispatch entity. Each sub-shipment independently satisfies BR-1.","B: Reject the feature as incompatible with the domain model. Require heavy shipments to be manually split by a Supervisor before dispatch.","C: Redefine BR-1 to allow many-to-many Shipment:Carrier relationships for split shipments. This is a fundamental domain model change with cascading impact."],"recommendation":"Option A. It preserves both invariants by modeling sub-shipments as first-class Shipment entities. The auto-split operation creates new Shipments (not modifies the existing one), and each new sub-shipment gets exactly one Carrier assigned. The Supervisor bypass question must still be resolved separately."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature explicitly bypasses Supervisor involvement for carrier assignment, directly violating BR-2 (only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier). Even if sub-shipments are modeled as new entities, the act of decomposing a Shipment that already has a locked Carrier assignment is effectively a reassignment — the original Carrier loses part of their assigned freight.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Carriers","Legal"],"options":["A: Require Supervisor approval for the auto-split operation itself, even if sub-shipment carrier assignment is automated. The Supervisor authorizes the split; the system assigns carriers.","B: Create a new authorization level — 'System Auto-Split' — that is permitted to assign carriers to new sub-shipments but cannot reassign existing ones. The original carrier keeps a sub-shipment for their portion.","C: Accept the bypass as intentional for this specific workflow, creating an exception to BR-2 for weight-triggered splits."],"recommendation":"Option A. The Supervisor reviews and approves the split decision, preserving human oversight for a high-impact operation. Carrier assignment within the approved split can then be automated within policy constraints."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to what is logically a single shipment","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses Supervisor involvement for carrier assignment/reassignment","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a Shipment"},{"violation":"The feature request does not define 'sub-shipment' as a domain concept, leaving the domain model in an inconsistent state","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 and BR-3: Without a formal sub-shipment entity, dispatch cannot guarantee one Carrier per dispatchable unit"}]}
